clusters.leadHeadline: Haslams Pay $205M for NWSL Expansion Team in Columbus
clusters.summary: NWSL adds 18th franchise with Columbus expansion backed by Haslam Sports Group. Expansion fee signals continued investor confidence and revenue growth in women's professional soccer.

clusters.leadHeadline: Illinois Lawmakers Race to Advance $5B Bears Stadium Plan
clusters.summary: State legislature accelerates votes on $5 billion Chicago Bears stadium financing ahead of potential relocation deadline. Deal hinges on state support as Bears ownership explores Indiana move option.

clusters.leadHeadline: NFL Rookie Deals Will Top $50M for First Time Since 2010
clusters.summary: 2026 top draft pick will earn nearly $55 million under new rookie scale agreement. Reflects inflated salary structure tied to record NFL media rights contracts.

clusters.leadHeadline: NIL Dispute Over 'Associated Entities' Could Break College Salary Cap
clusters.summary: Ongoing legal battle over NIL payment definitions threatens to eliminate any remaining salary cap constraints in college sports. Regulatory ambiguity creates competitive imbalance and compliance chaos.

clusters.leadHeadline: Churchill Downs Buys Preakness Stakes IP Rights for $85M
clusters.summary: Kentucky Derby operator secures exclusive intellectual property rights to historic Preakness Stakes. Consolidation under single ownership enables unified media distribution and sponsorship strategy.

clusters.leadHeadline: ESPN Defends NCAA Gymnastics Broadcast After Minnesota Backlash
clusters.summary: Network faces criticism for unequal airtime allocation during NCAA gymnastics coverage. Raises broader questions about broadcast equity and representation in women's collegiate sports media.

clusters.leadHeadline: Athlete-Founded Media Companies Compete for Sports Audience Share
clusters.summary: Athletes including Brandon Marshall launch independent media platforms to capture audience and monetize direct-to-consumer relationships. Reflects broader shift toward athlete ownership and content control.

sidebar.headline: Billy Donovan Leaves Bulls as Franchise Makeover Continues
NFL Defeats $27M Antitrust Suit Over Packers Songs
TickPick Acquires Momento App for Event Memories Platform
Spurs Fans Await Next Fiesta Shirt Night After 7-Year Playoff Gap

poll.question: Should colleges implement a hard salary cap on NIL payments to create parity?
options: Yes—level the playing field for all programs
No—NIL is free market, cap will get litigated away
Maybe—only if enforced by federal law

predictions.statement: Chicago Bears relocate to Indiana within 24 months after Illinois fails to pass stadium funding
predictions.rationale: Lawmakers racing against deadline suggests uncertainty; Bears have explored Indiana option as leverage tactic

predictions.statement: College sports NIL salary cap is eliminated entirely by end of 2026 due to antitrust litigation
predictions.rationale: Associated entities dispute ongoing; court precedent favors athlete compensation freedom over cap enforcement

predictions.statement: NWSL reaches 20 teams by 2028 with two more expansion awards at $200M+ per franchise
predictions.rationale: Columbus deal at $205M signals strong investor demand; league targets rapid growth trajectory

predictions.statement: Athlete-founded media platforms (I AM ATHLETE, Barstool talent) capture 15%+ of sports viewership by 2027
predictions.rationale: Early traction present but Netflix/traditional broadcast dominance remains; niche audience growth likely

predictions.statement: Trump administration uses federal funding enforcement to trigger college sports governance overhaul by Q3 2026
predictions.rationale: EO signals intent; regulatory threat could accelerate NIL rules standardization if funding leverage deployed
